claim: Vowel pattern '_.oii' is a robust plant-folio marker in the Voynich Manuscript. Across 115 plant-identified folios (Sherwood + Cohen, 4 conflicts excluded), '_.oii' appears at 4.84x the rate of 14 non-plant-ID herbal baseline folios (z=3.88). Five-fold cross-validation over plant folios shows 100% fold stability (all folds achieve enrichment >=1.5x, individual folds range 1.89x to 7.76x).
evidence_for: Full-dataset enrichment 4.84x with z=3.88 on 115 plant folios
Pattern discovered in v1 (3.71x on 19 folios), strengthened to 4.84x with 6x larger dataset — opposite of overfitting
Does not rely on lexicon matching or language identification
evidence_against: Baseline is thin (14 non-plant herbal folios) after plant-ID absorption
Fold 3 enrichment was only 1.89x — below fold 1 (5.12x) and fold 5 (7.76x); suggests some plant subsets carry the signal more strongly
Only 36 total plant-folio fires; a few miscounted plant IDs could materially shift the result
Pattern is herbal-section-biased; could reflect herbal-vs-other rather than plant-vs-non-plant within herbal
test: Replicate with alternative baseline: use pharmaceutical-section folios as negative control instead of non-plant herbal folios
test_threshold: >=2.0x enrichment vs pharma baseline: supported. <1.5x: herbal-section artefact.

tests_run.details: Enrichment 4.84x (z=3.88) full dataset; 100% 5-fold stability at >=1.5x threshold; pattern strengthened with 6x larger dataset rather than regressing to mean.

tests_remaining: Held-out test of sibling candidates o._.a (4.57x), o.eo (3.70x), _._._.o (3.36x)
Examine which specific plant species '_.oii' concentrates on — is it all plants, toxic plants, medicinal plants, a single botanical family?
reasoning: First plant-specific (not section-level) vowel-pattern finding to survive held-out validation. Pattern strengthens with more data (3.71x -> 4.84x), which is the OPPOSITE of overfitting. If the alternative-baseline test confirms, this is the first structurally-validated glyph-level marker tied to image-content.
